purpose: Experimental actuarial and quote-curve PoC for nomad acute emergency cover with prediction-market-style open risk backing.

quoteCurve.formula: unit_price(u) = base_unit_premium * risk_multiplier * reserve_stress * (1 + u / curve_depth) ^ gamma

quoteTables.label: Warm market after early nomad demand

scenarioResults.label: Mixed nomad launch: micro + standard budgets
Market-backed growth: open risk capital supports issuance
Thin-market growth: same demand without enough backers

hybridModelResults.name: Separated Backstop Curve
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Members buy capped cover from the curve while backers freely supply junior reserve capital.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 30-day acute emergency cover; choose any budget from 15 USD upward; quote returns a fixed cap up to 3000 USD.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Prediction-market feeling lives in open backer deposits and visible reserve depth, not in member claim promises.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Insurance-grade member entitlement; market-grade capital supply; no trader decides an individual claim.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
policyDesign: Member policy: choose budget, receive capped quote, activate entitlement.
Backer policy: deposit any amount into junior backstop capital.
Issuance rule: pause or reprice when reserve depth falls below target.

hybridModelResults.name: Calibrated Pay-Anything Curve
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Members can enter with any amount, but the curve converts that amount into an exact capped entitlement.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 30-day acute emergency cover; any budget above 15 USD is accepted and converted into a fixed cap before purchase.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: The bonding-curve output is coverage capacity, not a token; thin reserves or higher risk quote less cover per dollar.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: This is the good pay-anything version: flexible budget, fixed cap, reserve-gated issuance.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
Pay-anything is viable when the curve converts every dollar into an exact cap using current reserve depth.
The member budget is flexible, but the coverage promise is not flexible after purchase.
policyDesign: Member policy: pay any amount above the floor and receive a signed, fixed cap from the curve.
Pricing rule: the same 15 USD buys less cover when reserve depth is thin or cohort risk is higher.
Issuance rule: no entitlement mints unless p99.5 and reserve-floor gates pass after the quote.

hybridModelResults.name: Loss-Ratio Signal Market
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Members still buy capped insurance; traders separately price whether the pool loss ratio breaches a threshold.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: Same 30-day acute cover, with the pool quote curve nudged by a public aggregate-loss signal.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: A yes/no market on aggregate loss ratio above 65%; it produces a pricing signal but does not pay claims.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: The event market must settle on aggregate pool data only and be legally separate from the insurance promise.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
A prediction market is useful when it predicts aggregate pool risk instead of individual claim validity.
The event must be objective: for example, pool loss ratio above a published threshold after claim close.
policyDesign: Member policy: fixed 30-day acute emergency entitlement with signed quote receipt.
Market contract: aggregate pool loss ratio above threshold, settled after claim runout.
Claims boundary: AI processors and operators handle claims offchain; market data is aggregate only.
notes: Prediction market is modeled as a pricing and monitoring signal unless collateral is explicitly locked into a claims tranche.

hybridModelResults.name: Underwriting Prediction Tranche
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Predictors stake on aggregate pool risk, and a defined share of that stake becomes junior claims capital.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: Same 30-day acute cover from the quote curve; members never interact with individual claim markets.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Predictors forecast whether cohort loss ratio breaches a threshold; correct predictors earn yield, fees, surplus, and wrong-side penalties after claims gates.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Prediction collateral counts as reserve only for the explicitly locked claims tranche.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
Prediction collateral can count as claims capital only for the slice explicitly locked into the claims waterfall.
Correct predictors should earn from yield, fees, surplus, and wrong-side penalties only after claims reserves clear.
policyDesign: Member policy: same capped acute cover.
Market policy: predictors stake on aggregate loss-ratio bands; a fixed share of stake is posted as junior claims capital.
Reward rule: correct predictors earn only after claims, reserve margin, and capital replenishment are satisfied.
notes: 72576 USD of predictor collateral is treated as junior claims capital; 28224 USD remains trader payout liability.
Correct predictors are paid only after claims, reserve margin, and capital replenishment gates clear.

hybridModelResults.name: Collateralized Sidecar Vault
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Backers buy tranche-like risk shares that absorb pool losses in a transparent waterfall.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 30-day acute cover sold through the same quote curve; capacity expands when tranche capital clears.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Capital-market sidecar: junior and senior backers earn premium share for absorbing modeled claim volatility.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Closest to insurance-linked securities or collateralized reinsurance; requires strong offering restrictions.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
Backers should know exactly which layer of loss they absorb and when their capital can be released.
This is a capital-markets wrapper around insurance risk, not consumer gambling.
policyDesign: Member policy: same capped acute cover.
Capital policy: first-loss sponsor layer, junior sidecar layer, senior sidecar layer.
Release rule: capital unlocks only after claim runout, dispute window, and reserve reconciliation.
notes: Sponsor first loss: attaches at 0 USD, expected loss 86.92%, p99.5 impairment 100%.
Junior sidecar: attaches at 35000 USD, expected loss 1.01%, p99.5 impairment 15.19%.
Senior sidecar: attaches at 100000 USD, expected loss 0%, p99.5 impairment 0%.

hybridModelResults.name: Parametric Fast-Cash Overlay
hybridModelResults.oneLine: A small fixed cash benefit pays quickly when an independently verifiable acute event trigger fires.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 15 USD buys a 250 USD fast-cash benefit for ER admission or qualifying urgent-care trigger.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Backers price trigger frequency instead of open-ended medical bills; settlement is faster but has basis risk.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Can sit beside indemnity cover; trigger design must be explicit and not pretend to reimburse all losses.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
A fixed benefit is easier to price than open-ended reimbursement, but creates basis risk for members.
The trigger must be independently verifiable and hard to manipulate.
policyDesign: Member policy: small fixed cash benefit for a named urgent-care or ER trigger.
Claim rule: verified trigger pays fixed benefit without itemized bill reimbursement.
Disclosure: fast cash may be less than the medical bill and is not full health cover.
notes: Parametric benefit pays fast, but may underpay or overpay relative to the actual medical bill.

hybridModelResults.name: Member Mutual Rebate Pool
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Members buy capped cover and receive surplus rebates when the cohort has a good claims month.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 30-day capped cover with visible cohort economics; expected surplus funds member rebates and backer yield.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: The market element is a transparent surplus/rebate scoreboard, not speculative trading on claims.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: More consumer-friendly than a prediction market; still needs insurance-grade claim handling and rebate rules.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
Members can share upside from good claims experience without betting against sick members.
Rebates should be discretionary surplus distributions after reserves are satisfied.
policyDesign: Member policy: capped acute cover plus possible end-of-window surplus rebate.
Rebate rule: paid only after p99.5 reserve, claims runout, and fraud holds are cleared.
Backer rule: backers earn a defined surplus share before protocol margin.
notes: Rebate is modeled only from expected surplus; production would pay it after claim runout and reserve lock.

hybridModelResults.name: Member Health Bond
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Members can back themselves to avoid claims through an optional no-claim bond and rebate.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: 30-day capped acute cover plus optional health bond; no claim returns the bond and can earn a small rebate.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: The member is not betting to become sick; the bond reduces moral hazard and claim cost if a claim occurs.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: The bond is a member-aligned deductible/rebate layer, not a speculative market on individual sickness.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
A member can back themselves to avoid claims through a no-claim bond without profiting from being sick.
The bond should reduce net claim cost through forfeiture or deductible mechanics, not replace the coverage promise.
policyDesign: Member policy: capped cover plus optional no-claim bond.
Bond rule: no claim returns bond plus rebate; a covered claim forfeits the agreed amount into the pool.
Safety rule: members cannot buy uncapped upside from becoming sick.
notes: Health bonds are modeled as claim-cost mitigation and no-claim rebates, not as a way to profit from illness.

hybridModelResults.name: Full Stack Market Mutual
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Combines pay-anything coverage, sidecar reserve, predictor collateral, and optional member health bonds.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: Any budget above 15 USD quotes capped acute cover; members can optionally stake a no-claim bond.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Backers and predictors supply junior claims capital; correct predictors are paid from yield, fees, surplus, and wrong-side penalties after reserves clear.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Best long-term design, but only production-safe if reward and reserve waterfalls are separated in accounting.
firstPrinciples: A member promise must be fixed before the loss happens: named scope, window, cap, waits, and exclusions.
Claims stay in the AI/operator evidence workflow; markets price aggregate pool risk and reserve capacity.
Capacity is sold only while claims-paying reserve clears both stochastic loss and reserve-floor gates.
The strongest architecture combines flexible member pricing, explicit junior capital, predictor scoring, and no-claim incentives.
The UI can stay simple while the protocol separately accounts for reserve, market collateral, and member bond economics.
policyDesign: Member policy: pay-anything quote curve plus optional no-claim bond.
Capital policy: sidecar and prediction collateral form explicit junior reserve layers.
Settlement policy: claims first, reserve margin second, capital replenishment third, predictor/member rewards fourth.
notes: 140000 USD of predictor collateral is treated as junior claims capital; 60000 USD remains trader payout liability.
Correct predictors are paid only after claims, reserve margin, and capital replenishment gates clear.
Health bonds are modeled as claim-cost mitigation and no-claim rebates, not as a way to profit from illness.

hybridModelResults.name: Flat-Promise Pay-Anything Failure Case
hybridModelResults.oneLine: Bad curve red-team case: everyone pays any amount and receives the same broad cover promise.
hybridModelResults.memberOffering: Any payment from 15 USD upward is treated as full 3000 USD acute cover.
hybridModelResults.marketMechanism: Feels viral but collapses pricing: low-budget members buy the same liability as high-budget members.
hybridModelResults.productionBoundary: Use only as a red-team failure case; pay-anything must be calibrated into a fixed cap.
firstPrinciples: This failure case is a bad curve: every budget receives the same liability, so premium no longer tracks risk.
A viral pool is not the same thing as a solvent insurance product.
Pay-anything can work only when the amount paid maps to a fixed, reserve-gated cap before activation.
policyDesign: Do not ship this flat-promise version.
Use it only as a red-team case proving that the curve must bind budget to cap.
Never represent arbitrary payment as broad 3000 USD cover unless that cap is actually priced and reserved.
notes: This intentionally fails because pricing is no longer risk-proportional.

methodLimitations: This is a product-design and actuarial PoC, not an external actuarial opinion.
The model tests whether a low-budget quote curve can coexist with reserve-aware pricing and open risk-backer deposits.
Risk-backer deposits are modeled as junior/backstop reserve capital; they are not treated as member coverage purchases.
The member quote curve is an issuance and pricing model, not a commitment to offer blind pay-anything insurance.
Backer yield outputs are simple one-window surplus-share diagnostics, not promised returns.
Severity distributions are synthetic and should be replaced with credible paid-claim data before production.
